title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture
statement_text: If $F$ is a forest and $k \in \mathbb{N}$, then there exists an integer $c(F, k) \geq 1$ such that every graph $G$ excluding $F$ and $K_k$ as induced subgraphs satisfies $\chi(G) \leq c(F, k)$.
context_text: Described as maybe the most famous open problem in the area of chromatic number of graph classes defined by forbidden induced subgraphs. It can be equivalently restated as: $\mathrm{Forb_{ind}}(\mathcal{F})$ has bounded chromatic number if and only if $\mathcal{F}$ contains a clique and a forest. The paper studies directed analogues of this conjecture for digraphs.
paper_contribution: The paper studies directed extensions of this conjecture (Conjectures 1.4 and 1.5) and proves several special cases, explicitly framed as resolving special cases of an extension of the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture to directed graphs.
notes: PDF source. The paper studies directed analogues rather than the undirected conjecture directly; Conjecture 1.1 appears as background motivation.
paper_title: On coloring digraphs with forbidden induced subgraphs

title: Problem 1.2
statement_text: Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a finite set of digraphs. Under which circumstances does there exist $C \in \mathbb{N}$ such that every digraph $D$ without an induced subdigraph isomorphic to a member of $\mathcal{F}$ satisfies $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq C$?
context_text: Aboulker, Charbit and Naserasr initiated the systematic study of the relation between excluded induced subdigraphs and the dichromatic number, asking when the class $\mathrm{Forb_{ind}}(\mathcal{F})$ of digraphs has bounded dichromatic number — calling such $\mathcal{F}$ 'heroic'. This is the central overarching open problem motivating the paper.
paper_contribution: Proves several special cases contributing to the characterization of heroic sets of size 3, including resolving Conjecture 1.7 (Theorem 1), verifying Conjecture 1.4 for additional heroes (Theorems 3–4), and proving a new case of Conjecture 1.5 (Theorem 5).
paper_title: On coloring digraphs with forbidden induced subgraphs

title: Conjecture 1.4
statement_text: For every orientation of a star forest $F$ and every hero $H$ the oriented graphs excluding $F$ and $H$ as induced subdigraphs have bounded dichromatic number.
context_text: This is one of two oriented variants of the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture proposed by Aboulker, Charbit and Naserasr, arising from their classification of necessary conditions for heroic sets of size 3. It concerns triples of the form $\{\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, F, H\}$ where $F$ is an oriented star forest and $H$ is a hero.
paper_contribution: Proves special cases: Theorem 1 proves it for $F \in \{S_2^+, S_2^-\}$ and $H = \vec{C}_3$ (resolving Conjecture 1.7); Theorem 3 proves it for $F = S_2^+$ and $H = W_3^-$; Theorem 4 shows the property is preserved when replacing $H$ by the hero obtained by adding a dominating sink.
notes: PDF source. The conjecture is restated from [1] (Aboulker, Charbit, Naserasr) without an explicit citation in the theorem header, but the surrounding text makes the attribution clear.
paper_title: On coloring digraphs with forbidden induced subgraphs

title: Conjecture 1.5
statement_text: For every orientation of a forest $F$ and every $k \in \mathbb{N}$ the oriented graphs excluding $F$ and $\vec{K}_k$ as induced subdigraphs have bounded dichromatic number.
context_text: This is the second oriented variant of the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture from Aboulker et al., concerning heroic sets of size 3 of the form $\{\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, F, \vec{K}_k\}$ where $F$ is an oriented forest. By Proposition 1.6 proved in the paper, it suffices to establish the conjecture when $F$ is an oriented tree.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $F = P^+(1,1,1)$ (the alternating orientation of the path on four vertices) and $k = 3$ via Theorem 5, complementing an earlier result of Aboulker et al. for $F = \vec{P}_4$ and $k = 3$.
notes: PDF source. Attributed to [1] per surrounding text; no explicit citation appears in the theorem header.
paper_title: On coloring digraphs with forbidden induced subgraphs

title: Conjecture 1.7
context_text: This conjecture (Conjecture 6.2 in [1]) concerns the dichromatic number of directed-triangle-free oriented graphs in which every vertex's out-neighborhood (resp. in-neighborhood) induces a tournament. It is a special case of Conjecture 1.4 for $F \in \{S_2^+, S_2^-\}$ and $H = \vec{C}_3$, and was left open in [1] after heroicness was established for the three other 3-vertex star forests.
paper_contribution: Fully proved as Theorem 1; in fact, a strictly stronger result is proved as Theorem 2, namely $\vec{\chi}(\mathrm{Forbind}(\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, S_2^+, W_3^+)) = 2$, from which Conjecture 1.7 follows as an immediate corollary.
notes: PDF source. Explicitly labeled '(cf. [1], Conjecture 6.2)' in the paper.
paper_title: On coloring digraphs with forbidden induced subgraphs
